title: ABC: The command has to terminate. Boxes are not in a topological order.
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.51 (git sha1 c4b5190229616f7ebf8197f43990b4429de3e420)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

On my Debian GNU/Linux aarch64 machine, I tried this:

`yosys -l icesynth2.yosyslog -p "read_verilog -sv cpu2_nosv.v; synth_ice40 -top cpu -json icesynth2.json"`

[cpu2_nosv.v.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20054694/cpu2_nosv.v.gz)

### Expected Behavior

yosys synthesizes

### Actual Behavior

I get an error:

```
…
ABC: ** cmd error: aborting 'source <abc-temp-dir>/abc.script'
ABC: The command has to terminate. Boxes are not in a topological order.
ABC: The following information may help debugging (numbers are 0-based):
ABC: Input 0 of BoxA 5 (1stCI = 143; 1stCO = 21) has TFI with CI 992,
ABC: which corresponds to output 0 of BoxB 445 (1stCI = 992; 1stCO = 2097).
ABC: In a correct topological order, BoxB should precede BoxA.
ABC: Error: Abc_CommandAbc9If(): Mapping of GIA has failed.

2.41.16.6. Executing AIGER frontend.
ERROR: Can't open input file `/tmp/yosys-abc-y5d1HF/output.aig' for reading: No such file or directory
```

2.41.16.6. Executing AIGER frontend.
